page: Vultr voc-g-2c-8gb-50s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor. It is configured with 2 shared vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a local 50 GB NVMe SSD, and the server does not include any GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks show a mixed profile. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is in the top tier, reaching 3135.87. However, multi-core CPU performance drops to the average tier at 6213.65. Memory bandwidth is average for large blocks but weak for small cached blocks at 298.13 GB/sec. Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics, consistently rank in the bottom 25%. Database operations and static web serving throughput are poor, placing in the bottom 10% of benchmarks.

The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, which is reflected in the lower multi-core and database benchmark scores. This server offers a resource-dense, entry-level hardware profile suitable for lightweight workloads where high sustained multi-core performance is not critical. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, lightweight background tasks, or small-scale applications that benefit from fast local NVMe storage but do not require dedicated CPU resources.
description: The Vultr voc-g-2c-8gb-50s-amd is an entry-level server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 2 shared vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While stress-ng single-core benchmarks place this server in the top 10%, its multi-core performance and Passmark CPU scores fall into the average and weak categories, respectively. Database operations and static web serving throughput are poor, ranking in the bottom 10%. Due to its shared CPU allocation, this server is best suited for non-production environments, lightweight application hosting, and basic development tasks where cost efficiency is prioritized over dedicated, sustained multi-core compute power.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-2c-8gb-50s-amd is a shared-CPU server featuring 2 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe storage, suitable for lightweight workloads and development environments.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-2c-8gb-50s-amd is an x86_64 server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD storage for basic workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU instance with AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe storage for lightweight development and basic hosting workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC Milan x86_64 processor with 2 shared vCPUs
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance contrasted by weak multi-core and database benchmark results
Shared CPU allocation suitable for cost-sensitive, non-production environments
Appropriate for basic web serving, development tasks, and lightweight background processing
